verdict.answer_first: B states the prescription immediately: "start with a Postgres table (via a library, not hand-rolled)"; A opens with a conditional map: "if you already run Postgres... use a DB table... Reach for BullMQ... Use a cloud queue..."
verdict.decisiveness: B is more decisive because it gives one default, "DB table — the right default" and names tools; A ends by asking for more inputs before committing: "Tell me those and I'll commit to one."
verdict.fabrication: A invents/verifies less cautiously with "thousands of jobs/sec"; both use an unsupported threshold: "under a few hundred jobs/sec."
verdict.actionability: B is easier to execute because it converts the recommendation into a prescription: "Use pg-boss or graphile-worker" and "Don't write the SELECT ... loop yourself."
